title: 北アメリカのセミのライフサイクル

chart.caption: The diagram shows the life cycle of the North American cicada.
chart.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img' aria-label='図：セミのライフサイクル'><title>Cicada life cycle</title><circle cx='160' cy='100' r='70' fill='none' stroke='#c96442' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='6 6'/><g font-size='9' fill='#555' text-anchor='middle'><text x='160' y='34'>1. eggs in groove</text><text x='245' y='103'>2. nymph burrows</text><text x='160' y='178'>3. feeds on roots</text><text x='72' y='103'>4. sheds & mates</text></g><path d='M225,70 L235,60 L232,73 Z' fill='#c96442'/></svg>

model_paragraph: The diagram shows the life cycle of the North American cicada, which passes through several stages. After mating, the female saws a groove into the bark of a tree and lays her eggs. About eight weeks later, the larva, called a nymph, hatches and burrows down to the roots, where it feeds for 13 to 17 years. During this period it goes through four stages of development. On reaching adulthood, the cicadas emerge together, shed their exoskeleton, and the males look for a partner by making an incessant mating call. In this way, the cycle is renewed.

items.collocation: cut a groove into the bark
six phases of a life cycle

items.meaning: 再び始まる・繰り返される

drills.jp: セミのライフサイクルには複数の段階がある。
drills.model_en: The cicada's life cycle has several stages.

drills.jp: 幼虫は根に届くまで穴を掘る。
drills.model_en: The larva burrows down to reach the roots.

drills.jp: メスは木の樹皮に溝を作り、卵を産む。
drills.model_en: The female cuts a groove into the bark and lays her eggs.

drills.jp: 成虫は外骨格を脱ぎ捨てる。
オスは絶え間ない鳴き声で相手を探し、こうしてサイクルは再び始まる。
drills.model_en: The males look for a partner with an incessant call, and in this way the cycle is renewed.
